steps.title: Identify the feedback loop
steps.content: The strongest clue is in the example: geography influences sedimentary deposition, which over time alters coastal geography. This shows factors affecting each other in a circular, mutually-dependent relationship.

steps.title: Match the mutual dependence
steps.content:
The blank needs to describe factors that influence each other in both directions — the example shows geography affecting deposition and deposition altering geography. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — interdependent: means mutually reliant and influencing each other — exactly captures the two-way relationship described in the example. ✓
  • Choice B — unyielding: means rigid or inflexible — describes a quality of resistance, not mutual influence between factors. ✗
  • Choice C — tenuous: means weak or insubstantial — implies the factors barely connect, opposite to the strong mutual influence shown. ✗
  • Choice D — comprehensive: means all-inclusive or thorough — describes scope, not the feedback-loop relationship between the factors. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
